title: FAA approves military use of drone-killing laser weapons in US airspace decision comes after it was decided systems do not present an increased risk to the flying public
summary: The FAA has granted approval for military laser systems to shoot down drones in US airspace, deeming them safe for public use.
insight: Military's ability to counter rogue drones enhances national airspace security.

title: Nvidia says AI cuts 10-month, eight-engineer GPU design task to overnight job company is still 'a long way' from AI designing chips without human input
summary: Nvidia uses AI to speed up GPU design, cutting 10 months to overnight. However, the company still requires human input. AI is not yet autonomous in designing chips.
insight: AI accelerates chip design, but human oversight remains crucial.

title: Small Missouri town ousts half its city council after $6 billion AI data center approval petition calls for mayor's removal as frustration (and violence) over AI data centers mounts
summary: Voters in Festus, MO ousted half of the city council members after a $6 billion AI data center project was approved, sparking frustration and calls for the mayor's removal.
insight: Community pushback against AI data centers reflects growing local distrust.

title: New tech can see a CPU's transistors in action terahertz radiation can potentially steal data as a chip is running
summary: Researchers have developed a method to observe individual CPU transistors in action using terahertz radiation. This technology allows for a deeper understanding of chip performance. It can also potentially be used to steal sensitive data.
insight: Terahertz radiation can be used for both observation and data theft.

title: Intel's Nova Lake CPUs gear up to seize AMD s 3D V-Cache gaming throne early leak points to up to 52 cores, blazing DDR5-8000 support, and massive 175W TDP
summary: Intel's Nova Lake CPUs may feature up to 52 cores and support DDR5-8000 speeds, with a massive 175W TDP. The Core Ultra 400S processors are expected to compete with AMD's 3D V-Cache gaming lineup. Details are based on an early leak of the CPU's preliminary SKU list.
insight: Intel's massive core count could surpass AMD's 3D V-Cache performance

title: Acer Predator GX850 SFX power supply review: Solid electrical performance with good efficiency
summary: The Acer Predator GX850 SFX power supply offers reliable electrical performance with good efficiency. It's compact design is a result of solid engineering from FSP. However, its price and noise levels may present practical challenges.
insight: Compact SFX design for PC builds balances power and noise trade-offs.

title: NZXT to cough up $3.45 million over 'predatory' Flex PC rental scheme in RICO class-action settlement in-debt customers to get up to $5,000 of relief, eligible renters to be granted ownership
summary: NZXT agreed to pay $3.45 million in a RICO class-action settlement. Eligible customers may receive up to $5,000 in relief. Renters may be granted ownership.
insight: Predatory business practices target vulnerable groups, including children and in-debt customers.

title: Bulbous 15x fan PC case side panel dubbed the Superdome lowers temps by 20 degrees $600 worth of Noctua fans arrayed in 3D-printed structure
summary: The Superdome, a unique 15x fan PC case side panel, has shown improved cooling performance. Constructed with $600 worth of Noctua fans in a 3D-printed structure, it lowers temperatures by 20 degrees. This innovative design presents a promising solution for high-heat computing.
insight: Innovative cooling designs can significantly reduce PC temperature and improve performance.

title: Approvals for Nvidia and AMD AI chip exports to China stall under government bottleneck 20% staff turnover hobbles Bureau of Industry and Security
summary: U.S. Commerce Department's Bureau of Industry and Security is severely understaffed, hindering AI chip exports to China due to a 20% staff turnover rate over the past year.
insight: Weak export vetting exposes U.S. AI tech to potential security risks

title: Espresso Lite 15 Review: An entry-level portable monitor with a splash of color
summary: The Espresso Lite 15 is an entry-level portable monitor offering color and portability, perfect for creators on-the-go.
insight: Innovative portable display for creative professionals on a budget

title: 'CopprLink' destroys every eGPU standard in new test, achieves near-native-level performance with an RTX 5090 setup requires $2,300 worth of additional hardware
summary: CopprLink eGPU standard achieves near-native performance with RTX 5090 setup. This is made possible by a $1,300 enclosure and a $1,000 adapter card. The setup is geared towards enterprise markets but showcases the potential for future consumer eGPUs.
insight: eGPU technology can rival motherboard performance with advanced adapters.

title: Website backup crippled by 1.6MB Friends GIF that was replicated 246,173 times, breaking Linux's EXT4 filesystem limit Jennifer Aniston's 'happy dance' animation ate up 377 gigabytes of data due to security policy
summary: A single 1.6MB GIF replicated 246,173 times caused a website backup to reach 377GB due to Linux's EXT4 file system limit. This limitation led to issues with the site's backup quota. The incident highlights the potential for small, duplicated files to cause significant storage issues.
insight: Small, duplicated files can silently cripple storage capacity.

title: Why we spent 50+ hours retesting Intel s Core Ultra 270K Plus and 250K Plus
summary: We retested Intel's Core Ultra 270K Plus and 250K Plus after initial surprising results, spending over 50 hours to validate the data.
insight: High-performance CPU results require rigorous testing to build trust in benchmarks.

title: 192 MHz WCH CH32V205 RISC-V MCU offers a 480 Mbps USB 2.0 interface
summary: The WCH CH32V205 is a 192 MHz RISC-V MCU with 480 Mbps USB 2.0 interface and various peripherals, including 80 GPIOs and a 16-channel 12-bit ADC.
insight: High-performance RISC-V MCU with advanced USB and peripherals capabilities.

title: DeskUp Pro smart standing desk controller integrates with Home Assistant and Homey Pro Smart Home hubs
summary: The DeskUp Pro is a standing desk controller that integrates with Home Assistant and Homey Pro, allowing users to automate desk height, set reminders, and control their desk with voice assistants. It plugs directly into the desk's RJ11/RJ12 port and supports Jiecang control boxes. This solution enhances smart home functionality for standing desk users.
insight: Streamlines smart home integration for standing desk users effortlessly

title: Trail Mate open-source firmware leverages Meshtastic and MeshCore for ESP32 off-grid handhelds
summary: Trail Mate is an open-source firmware for off-grid ESP32 handhelds, providing GPS mapping, LoRa messaging, and offline capabilities.
insight: Enabling off-grid communication and navigation without relying on smartphones.

title: With Artemis safely home, NASA eyes radiation for future flights
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission has successfully returned Orion to Earth, equipped with radiation sensors. These sensors will inform future mission planning, once testing on astronaut tissue chips is complete. The data collected will help protect astronauts from radiation exposure.
insight: Protecting astronauts from radiation exposure is crucial for long-duration spaceflight

title: Microsoft admits Game Pass may have gotten too expensive
summary: Microsoft's Xbox division acknowledges Game Pass has become too expensive. The company is internally exploring lower-cost subscription tiers. No firm timeline is set for these changes.
insight: Pricing strategy shift may impact Game Pass's market dominance

title: Denuvo removed from Resident Evil Requiem, improving performance over hypervisor-based crack
summary: Voices38 has removed Denuvo from Resident Evil Requiem, improving its performance. This marks the first game of 2026 to be fully cracked. The move is seen as a significant development in gaming.
insight: Denuvo's removal can significantly enhance game performance post-cracking.

title: What was the first ransomware attack to demand payment in Bitcoin?
summary: The first known ransomware attack to demand payment in Bitcoin occurred in 2013. It was a variant of the 'CryptoLocker' malware. The attackers demanded payment in Bitcoin to restore encrypted files.
insight: Established Bitcoin as a preferred currency for ransomware attackers worldwide.

title: Stop Killing Games backs California bill supporting clearer end-of-life rules for online games
summary: Stop Killing Games supports California's Protect Our Games Act, requiring clear long-term support for online games. The bill aims to ensure gaming companies commit to server-connected game support. This legislation aims to give players clarity on game longevity.
insight: Transparency on game longevity boosts player trust and loyalty.

title: Australia's social media ban for kids mostly isn't working, research suggests
summary: Australia's social media ban for users under 16 isn't effective, research shows. The ban aimed to protect young users from online risks, but kids are still accessing platforms. The government had hoped for better results from the ban.
insight: Children are finding ways to circumvent age restrictions on social media

title: Linux 7.0 released with self-healing XFS and expanded support for AMD, Intel, and ARM64 hardware
summary: Linux 7.0 introduces official support for Rust, marking a significant development milestone. This move is part of the 'Rust Experiment' that began in 2020. The release also includes self-healing XFS and expanded hardware support.
insight: Linux's adoption of Rust enhances reliability and security standards.

title: OBS Studio revamps audio mixer, adds WebRTC simulcast support
summary: OBS Studio 32.1 revamps its audio mixer with a new design and features, and introduces WebRTC simulcast support.
insight: Enhanced streaming capabilities for improved audio and video quality control.

title: NZXT to pay $3.45 million in class-action settlement over "predatory" Flex PC rental scheme
summary: NZXT agreed to a $3.45 million settlement over its Flex PC rental program, which was accused of using predatory and bait-and-switch tactics.
insight: Gamers are wary of companies with questionable business practices and loyalty.

title: MicroProse's next game is flight simulator Legendary Pilots
summary: MicroProse's Legendary Pilots is a modern indie flight simulator with management elements. The game aims to deliver a complete yet accessible experience. It's set to be released by the end of the month.
insight: Reviving flight simulation gaming with accessible management features

title: After Fortnite stumbles and layoffs, Epic bets on a Disney extraction shooter
summary: Epic Games faces uncertainty after layoffs and game updates underperform. The company is shifting focus to a new Disney extraction shooter. Fortnite will see further game mode closures.
insight: Epic diversifies to mitigate Fortnite's decline in popularity

title: Valve engineer develops Linux VRAM fix that boosts gaming on 8GB GPUs
summary: Valve engineer Natalie Vock develops Linux patches to boost gaming performance on 8GB GPUs by prioritizing game VRAM for faster local memory access.
insight: Prioritizing game VRAM improves gaming performance on lower spec GPUs significantly.

title: Study finds even the best AI models lose money betting on soccer
summary: A London-based AI start-up found top-tier models consistently lost money predicting Premier League match outcomes.
insight: Even top AI models struggle in dynamic environments like sports forecasting.

title: South Korea to give 7 million mobile users free unlimited data after hitting monthly caps
summary: South Korea's three major carriers will provide free unlimited data to 7 million users after hitting monthly caps.
insight: Promotes digital equity and reduces financial burden for heavy data users.

title: Iran's internet blackout passes 1,000 hours as connectivity drops to near zero
summary: Iran's internet blackout has surpassed 43 days, with connectivity at 1% pre-blackout levels.
insight: Mass internet censorship severely restricts information access and free speech.

title: Everything is becoming agentic, and we're not fully ready for it
summary: Emerging AI tools are rapidly automating tasks and transforming industries.
insight: Rise of autonomous tools challenges human skills and adaptability slowly.

title: Sam Altman's San Francisco home targeted by Molotov cocktail and gunfire in two separate attacks
summary: Sam Altman's San Francisco home was targeted by Molotov cocktail and gunfire in two separate attacks. A 20-year-old man was arrested and booked into jail on various charges.
insight: High-profile figures' homes increasingly vulnerable to violent attacks nationwide.
